county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Genesee County in 1824. Lake Ontario county. Agricultural region known as 'The Cobblestone Region' for unique stone construction. Erie Canal passes through.
county_metadata.research_tips: Orleans County Clerk in Albion holds deed records from 1824 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1824 records in Genesee County. The county is known for its cobblestone buildings and the Cobblestone Society Museum in Childs preserves architectural and local history. The Orleans County Historical Association in Albion maintains genealogical files and cemetery records. The Erie Canal brought settlers and commerce through Albion and Medina. For agricultural research, the Orleans County apple and fruit industry records document orchard families. Many settlers came from New England via the Erie Canal. The Medina sandstone quarry industry generated unique employment records. Lee-Whedon Memorial Library in Medina has local history collections.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1824; county clerk Albion

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Oneida and Onondaga Counties in 1816. Lake Ontario port city. Fort Ontario (from 1755). Major grain shipping port. Fulton and Pulaski are other towns.
county_metadata.research_tips: Oswego County Clerk holds deed records from 1816 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1816 records split between Oneida and Onondaga Counties. Fort Ontario (active from 1755) has military records from French and Indian War through WWII at NARA and the fort museum. The Oswego County Historical Society in Oswego has genealogical files and port records. Oswego was a major Lake Ontario grain shipping port and waterfront employment records document dock workers. The Oswego SUNY campus Richardson-Bates House Museum has local history. The county's Safe Haven emergency refugee camp (1944) housed European refugees and records are at NARA. Fulton and Pulaski have separate historical societies with local records. For French Canadian families, check Catholic parish records in the northern towns bordering Jefferson County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1816; county clerk Oswego

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1791. Home of James Fenimore Cooper (Cooperstown). Baseball Hall of Fame. Otsego Lake. Agricultural and dairy farming county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Otsego County Clerk in Cooperstown holds deed records from 1791 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1791 records in Montgomery County. Cooperstown is a major genealogical destination — the New York State Historical Association (Fenimore Art Museum) has outstanding manuscript collections including Cooper family papers, Cherry Valley records, and regional agricultural records. The Otsego County Historical Society has genealogical files. Cherry Valley was site of a 1778 massacre and military records document the event and affected families. The county was settled by New Englanders, particularly from Connecticut. Hartwick Seminary records (from 1797) document Lutheran families. Oneonta State has local history collections. The county's dairy and hop-growing industries generated agricultural society records. Fly Creek and other small town historical societies have excellent local records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1791; county clerk Cooperstown

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Dutchess County in 1812. Lower Hudson Valley county. Named for Gen. Israel Putnam. Smallest county in NY by area (after NYC boroughs). Suburban/exurban character.
county_metadata.research_tips: Putnam County Clerk in Carmel holds deed records from 1812 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1812 records in Dutchess County. The Putnam County Historical Society (Foundry School Museum in Cold Spring) maintains genealogical files and local histories. The West Point Foundry records in Cold Spring document iron workers. Cold Spring was a major iron foundry town producing Parrott guns for the Civil War. The Putnam County Historian's office has cemetery records and genealogical collections. For colonial families, Philipstown and Southeast town records predate county formation. Putnam Valley Historical Society has records for the interior communities. The county's proximity to NYC made it a summer retreat and resort records are at local historical societies. Southeast town records include early Quaker meeting records important for Dutchess-Putnam border families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1812; county clerk Carmel

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires. Records well preserved.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Coterminous with Borough of Queens (NYC 1898). Nassau County split off in 1899. Most ethnically diverse urban area in the world.
county_metadata.research_tips: Queens County records are split between the Queens County Clerk (deed records from 1683) and NYC Municipal Archives (vital records). Birth records at Municipal Archives from 1898 (for the borough); earlier births under individual town records or church records. The Queens County Clerk in Jamaica has deed records from 1683. Queens Historical Society has genealogical files and local history. Before consolidation (1898), Queens was predominantly rural with towns like Flushing, Jamaica, Newtown, and Long Island City having separate records. The Flushing Quaker Meeting House (1694) has some of the oldest continuous religious records in NY. The Bowne House in Flushing documents religious freedom history. Queens' extraordinary ethnic diversity since 1960s means community organization records in multiple languages. LaGuardia and JFK airport employment records document 20th-century workers. Pre-1899, Queens included present-day Nassau County, so colonial records may cover both areas.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1683 colonial era; Queens County Clerk Jamaica

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany County in 1791. Named for Van Rensselaer patroonship. Troy is county seat. Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute (RPI). Collar City (shirt manufacturing). Anti-rent movement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rensselaer County Clerk in Troy holds deed records from 1791 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1791 records in Albany County. The county was part of the Van Rensselaer patroonship and tenant farming records are extensive. The Rensselaer County Historical Society in Troy has outstanding genealogical collections including family files, Troy city directories from 1829, and industrial records. Troy was the 'Collar City' and shirt manufacturing employment records document thousands of women workers. The anti-rent movement (1839-1845) tenant farmer records are at NYS Archives. RPI archives have student and institutional records from 1824. The Hart-Cluett House Museum has Troy merchant family papers. For Dutch colonial families, the Lansingburgh and Schaghticoke Reformed Church records are essential. Uncle Sam originated in Troy (Samuel Wilson, War of 1812) and Troy Historical Society has Wilson family records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1791; county clerk Troy

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Van Rensselaer patroonship records significant.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant record losses. Records well preserved from colonial period.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Coterminous with Borough of Staten Island (NYC 1898). Connected to Brooklyn by Verrazano Bridge (1964). Rural character preserved longer than other NYC boroughs.
county_metadata.research_tips: Richmond County (Staten Island) records are split between the Richmond County Clerk and NYC Municipal Archives. The County Clerk has deed records from 1683. The Municipal Archives holds birth (1898+), death (1898+), and marriage records. Pre-1898 vital records are in town clerk records and church records. The Staten Island Historical Society at Historic Richmond Town has outstanding genealogical collections including family files, Dutch colonial records, and farming community records. The Staten Island Museum has manuscript collections. Dutch Reformed church records from the colonial era are essential — Port Richmond Reformed Church and other congregations. The Huguenot community on Staten Island is documented in Reformed Church records. Staten Island retained rural character through the early 1900s, so agricultural records persist later here than in other NYC boroughs. The Conference House (Billopp House, 1680) records document the oldest surviving dwelling in NYC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1683 colonial era; Richmond County Clerk

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Orange County in 1798. Lower Hudson Valley, west bank. Smallest NY county by area (excluding NYC boroughs). Tappan was site of Major Andre trial. Ramapo Mountains.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rockland County Clerk in New City holds deed records from 1798 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1798 records in Orange County. The Historical Society of Rockland County in New City has genealogical collections. Tappan was the site of Major Andre's trial and execution (1780) and the DeWint House (Washington's headquarters) has Revolutionary War records. The county has significant Jewish community records (post-1950s settlement). The Ramapo Mountain People (Ramapough Lenape) have their own heritage and records. African American community in Nyack documented through AME church records. Palisades Interstate Park archives have regional land history. The county's rapid suburbanization after WWII and the Tappan Zee Bridge (1955) transformed demographics. Pearl River and surrounding communities have strong Irish American heritage documented in local historical societies.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1798; county clerk New City

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Clinton County in 1802. Largest county in NY by area. Northern border with Canada on St. Lawrence River. Canton is county seat. Potsdam, Massena, Ogdensburg are other centers. SUNY Canton and Clarkson University.
county_metadata.research_tips: St. Lawrence County Clerk in Canton holds deed records from 1802 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1802 records in Clinton County. As the largest county in NY, St. Lawrence has diverse records across widely scattered communities. The St. Lawrence County Historical Association in Canton has excellent genealogical collections including family files, cemetery records, and immigration documentation. The county attracted diverse immigrants: French Canadians, Irish, Scots, and Swedes. Ogdensburg was a major St. Lawrence River port with customs and shipping records. Massena was the site of Alcoa and Reynolds aluminum plants with extensive employment records. The St. Regis Mohawk Reservation (Akwesasne) extends into St. Lawrence County and tribal records are separate. Clarkson University archives have regional history. The county's iron mining (in the east) and dairy farming generated industry-specific records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1802; county clerk Canton
